mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor market conditions compress wage growth slack, triggering inflationary pressures that force the Federal Reserve to pause rate cuts, thereby capping equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE is below 4.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion (RSAFS) combined with rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals synchronized demand-pull growth, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC greater than 0.05, or periods of high RSAFS and high INDPRO yield negative average forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs for highly leveraged firms, but if housing starts (HOUST) remain resilient, it indicates robust underlying demand that offsets rate sensitivity, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when FEDFUNDS rises and HOUST remains above 1.2 million, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects expanding economic activity, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth is positive but 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling correlation between GDP growth and SPX returns drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5% annualized, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPI annualized growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth (PAYEMS) coupled with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market that boosts household income and consumption, fueling earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict 5-day returns with statistical significance, or periods of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE coincide with negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain constraints, rising industrial production (INDPRO) alongside flat housing starts (HOUST) indicates capital is being diverted to manufacturing over residential construction, boosting industrial sector profits and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises and HOUST remains flat, or the interaction effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.
